 {"id":"bd-3j1","title":"Docs: Proof report (security/perf vs Node/Bun)","description":"# Goal
Produce a **self-contained proof report** that demonstrates why PiJS is more secure and performant than Node/Bun for extensions.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Security argument: capability model, audit logs, least-privilege connectors.
- Determinism argument: event loop spec + conformance evidence.
- Performance evidence: benchmark tables + flamegraph notes.
- Compatibility matrix: supported APIs + rewrite map summary.
- Hybrid story: QuickJS for JS + wasmtime for wasm (PiWasm bridge) without Node/Bun.
- Repro steps: exact commands and expected artifacts.

# Hard requirement
- The report must cite evidence that **all 16 extensions in `docs/extension-sample.json` run unmodified** under the harness.

# Audience
- Skeptics who expect Node/Bun.
- Future maintainers who need the rationale baked in.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
- [ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead
- [ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
- [ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
- [ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
- [ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes

[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:23:56.576983634Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T06:42:51.022104442Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T06:42:44.999892485Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3j1","depends_on_id":"bd-193","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:08Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3j1","depends_on_id":"bd-1pb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:08Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3j1","depends_on_id":"bd-2dd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:08Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3j1","depends_on_id":"bd-2ki","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:08Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3j1","depends_on_id":"bd-331","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:08Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3j1","depends_on_id":"bd-3bs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:08Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3j1","depends_on_id":"bd-hyg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:08Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3j1","depends_on_id":"bd-w83","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:08Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":3206,"issue_id":"bd-3j1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Add to proof report (talking points + citations)

- Existence proof: `txiki.js` (QuickJS-ng + libuv) demonstrates a non-Node/Bun event loop + OS wrapper layer around QuickJS is feasible.
- Stronger claim: PiJS intentionally rejects ambient authority; all I/O is capability-gated connectors with audit logs.
- Cite QuickJS docs: bytecode is version-coupled and not security-checked; therefore PiJS treats bytecode as local cache only.
- Cite quickjs-emscripten (or equivalent): embedder must drain job queue (`executePendingJobs`) to run microtasks.
- Argue determinism + security + performance are jointly achievable (and measurable) because the API surface is small and explicit.
","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:04:16Z"},{"id":3207,"issue_id":"bd-3j1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Primary citations (for bd-3j1 proof report)

1) QuickJS docs (bytecode safety + version coupling)
- QuickJS warns bytecode is version-coupled and not safety-checked before execution.
- Ref: https://bellard.org/quickjs/quickjs.html

2) QuickJS embedder-driven microtask/job draining
- quickjs-libc provides a reference loop (js_std_loop) that polls OS and drains pending jobs via JS_ExecutePendingJob.
- Ref: https://cs.opensource.google/fuchsia/fuchsia/+/master:third_party/quickjs/quickjs-libc.c

3) Existence proof for “QuickJS + event loop + OS wrappers without Node/Bun”
- txiki.js (QuickJS-ng + libuv)
- Ref: https://github.com/saghul/txiki.js
","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:22Z"},{"id":3208,"issue_id":"bd-3j1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Closed. Created docs/PIJS_PROOF_REPORT.md: security argument (capability model, 30 negative tests), compatibility evidence (187/223 pass, Node shims, txiki.js proof), determinism (formal event loop, LabRuntime, property tests), performance (cold P95=106ms, warm P99<1ms), comparison table (Node vs Bun vs PiJS), full reproduction steps.","created_at":"2026-02-07T06:42:51Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3j4f","title":"Rustdoc: audit public API surface (decide what is truly public)","description":"# Goal
Clarify the intended public API of the `pi` crate so rustdoc work is focused and we don’t accidentally commit to unstable internals.

# Scope
- Inventory `pub` modules/types in `src/lib.rs` and key modules.
- Decide for each:
  - should remain `pub` and documented as supported
  - should become `pub(crate)` / private
  - should be re-exported from a smaller facade module

# Why
A smaller, intentional public surface reduces maintenance burden and makes rustdoc more meaningful.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A short list of "public" modules/types exists (can be a comment in `src/lib.rs` or a doc note).
- [ ] Clear decisions recorded for what is internal vs public.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented

[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T21:23:59.407524690Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:28:52.363252116Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T08:12:53.305690224Z","close_reason":"Completed: added public API policy note in src/lib.rs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3j4f","depends_on_id":"bd-14od","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3ja5","title":"Task: Create ExtensionToolWrapper implementing Tool trait","description":"# Task: Create ExtensionToolWrapper

## Objective

Create a Rust struct that wraps an extension-registered tool, implementing the Tool trait so it can be used in the agent's tool registry.

## Implementation

```rust
/// Wrapper for extension-registered tools.
/// 
/// This struct implements the Tool trait, allowing extension tools
/// to be used alongside built-in tools in the agent's registry.
pub struct ExtensionToolWrapper {
    def: ExtensionToolDef,
    runtime: Arc<PiJsRuntime>,
}

impl ExtensionToolWrapper {
    pub fn new(def: ExtensionToolDef, runtime: Arc<PiJsRuntime>) -> Self {
        Self { def, runtime }
    }
}

#[async_trait]
impl Tool for ExtensionToolWrapper {
    fn name(&self) -> &str {
        &self.def.name
    }
    
    fn label(&self) -> &str {
        &self.def.label
    }
    
    fn description(&self) -> &str {
        &self.def.description
    }
    
    fn parameters(&self) -> serde_json::Value {
        self.def.parameters.clone()
    }
    
    async fn execute(
        &self,
        tool_call_id: &str,
        input: serde_json::Value,
        _on_update: Option<Box<dyn Fn(ToolUpdate) + Send>>,
    ) -> Result<ToolOutput> {
        // Call into JS runtime to execute the tool
        self.runtime.execute_extension_tool(
            &self.def.name,
            tool_call_id,
            input,
        ).await
    }
}
```

## JS Bridge Method

Need corresponding method in PiJsRuntime:

```rust
impl PiJsRuntime {
    /// Execute an extension-registered tool.
    pub async fn execute_extension_tool(
        &self,
        name: &str,
        call_id: &str,
        input: serde_json::Value,
    ) -> Result<ToolOutput> {
        let input_json = serde_json::to_string(&input)?;
        
        // Call JS handler
        let result = self.eval(&format!(
            "__pi_execute_tool({}, {}, {})",
            serde_json::to_string(name)?,
            serde_json::to_string(call_id)?,
            input_json
        )).await?;
        
        // Wait for async result (tool handlers are async)
        // This requires Promise resolution via the hostcall mechanism
        // The tool handler calls back via the Promise bridge
        
        // Parse result
        self.await_tool_result(call_id).await
    }
}
```

## Async Tool Execution

Extension tools are async in JS:
```javascript
registerTool({
    name: "my-tool",
    execute: async (callId, input) => {
        // Async work...
        return { content: [...], details: {...} };
    }
});
```

The execution flow:
1. Rust calls __pi_execute_tool(name, callId, input)
2. JS looks up handler and calls it (returns Promise)
3. Promise resolves to tool result
4. Result marshaled back to Rust

## Testing

1. Unit test: Wrapper implements Tool trait correctly
2. Unit test: execute() routes to JS
3. Unit test: JS result parsed correctly
4. Integration test: Full tool invocation via wrapper

## Dependencies

- Depends on: bd-254u (get_registered_tools)
- Depends on: bd-jt3k (Agent loop integration)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] ExtensionToolWrapper struct created
- [ ] Implements Tool trait
- [ ] execute() calls into JS runtime
- [ ] Results parsed correctly
- [ ] Unit tests pass","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:59:32.890594336Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T21:40:44.624766948Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T21:40:44.624668705Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ja5","depends_on_id":"bd-1yo9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3jg7","title":"Conformance: Tier 1 - Minimal official extensions (14 exts)","description":"# Conformance: Tier 1 - Minimal official extensions (14 exts)

## Extensions
hello, pirate, session-name, custom-footer, custom-header, system-prompt-header, preset, titlebar-spinner, status-line, model-status, widget-placement, question, qna, send-user-message

## What These Test
- Basic extension loading and factory function execution
- Simple registrations: single command, single event handler
- UI header/footer/widget placement
- Session name get/set
- Simple message sending

## Mock Requirements
Minimal mocks needed. Default mock spec should suffice:
- Session: basic message history
- UI: capture mode (no rendering)

## Expected Behavior
These are the simplest extensions. If ANY of these fail, there is a fundamental runtime bug. 100% pass rate is mandatory.

## Key Files per Extension
- hello/hello.ts: registers /hello command, shows greeting
- pirate/pirate.ts: registers input transform, modifies messages
- session-name/session-name.ts: uses getSessionName/setSessionName
- custom-footer/custom-footer.ts: registers footer component
- custom-header/custom-header.ts: registers header component

## Debugging Strategy
For any failure:
1. Check TS output: does it load? What does it register?
2. Check Rust output: does it load? What does it register?
3. Diff the two outputs
4. Most likely issue: missing hostcall implementation, wrong return type, or swc compilation issue

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 14 extensions pass differential comparison
- 100% pass rate (no exceptions)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:23:08.773055794Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T17:55:09.443364334Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T17:55:09.443196412Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3jg7","depends_on_id":"bd-150s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3jg7","depends_on_id":"bd-21dv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:04Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-m9rk","title":"No-mock: extensions message/session tests use real Session","description":"# Goal
Remove in-memory/session mocks from extension message/session tests and use real `Session` + `SessionHandle` paths.

# Background
`tests/extensions_message_session.rs` defines an in-memory session mock and `tests/e2e_message_session_control.rs` uses `load_extension_with_mocks`. This conflicts with the no-mock policy for core paths; we should exercise the real session plumbing and serialization behavior instead.

# Scope
- Replace in-memory session mock with real `Session` + `SessionHandle` backed by a temp directory.
- Use real extension fixtures (from `tests/ext_conformance/artifacts/**`) instead of mock extension strings where feasible.
- Validate session mutation APIs: append message, set label/name, add custom entry, get branch entries.
- Emit JSONL logs + artifacts (session JSONL, fixture id, event sequence).

# Files
- `tests/extensions_message_session.rs`
- `tests/e2e_message_session_control.rs`
- `tests/ext_conformance/artifacts/**` (fixtures)

# Acceptance
- Tests run against real session persistence (JSONL) and real extension artifacts.
- No in-memory mock session types remain in these tests, unless explicitly allowlisted with rationale in `docs/TEST_COVERAGE_MATRIX.md`.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:26:46.911824942Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T07:34:14.146134401Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T07:34:14.146002815Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m9rk","depends_on_id":"bd-102","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-mjxes","title":"Session picker fallback ignores SQLite WAL freshness","description":"Fresh-eyes review found that src/session_index.rs now accounts for -wal/-shm sidecars, but src/session_picker.rs::build_meta_from_sqlite() still measures only the primary .sqlite file during fallback disk scans.  {"id":"bd-nh33","title":"E2E logs: extension registration JSONL + artifacts","description":"# Goal
Add JSONL log + artifact index output for extension registration E2E tests.

# Background
`tests/e2e_extension_registration.rs` currently validates registration but does not emit JSONL logs/artifacts. We need detailed, deterministic logs per bd-4u9 for CI diffing and diagnosis.

# Scope
- Wire `TestHarness` logging to emit JSONL logs and artifact index for each test case.
- Capture key artifacts: extension source used, registration payloads, provider list snapshot, and any generated session files.
- Include redaction summary and deterministic ordering.

# Files
- `tests/e2e_extension_registration.rs`
- `tests/common/harness.rs` (only if helper additions are needed)

# Acceptance
- JSONL logs + artifact index are produced for all tests in this file.
- Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary (bd-4u9).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:31:02.522793845Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T08:09:26.458628477Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T08:09:26.458536686Z","close_reason":"JSONL log + artifact index output already present in e2e_extension_registration.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Will post update once all fmt/check/clippy/test (incl. wasm-host) are green.","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:29:20Z"},{"id":3095,"issue_id":"bd-nom","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"wasmtime component model reference

- wasmtime component API docs (WIT/component instantiation patterns):
  https://docs.wasmtime.dev/api/wasmtime/component/index.html

 {"id":"bd-nom.1","title":"Hostcall ABI: WASM host.call Uses Shared Dispatcher (Parity Refactor)","description":"# Goal
Refactor the WASM component hostcall path (`host.call(name, input_json)` in `src/extensions.rs` `mod wasm_host`) so it uses the shared hostcall dispatcher:

`HostCallPayload (from JSON) -> dispatch_host_call_shared() -> HostResultPayload -> WIT return string`

# Current State (Observed)
- WASM hostcalls are implemented in `src/extensions.rs` under `mod wasm_host`, in `impl host::Host for HostState { async fn call(...) }`.
- The current implementation:
  - parses JSON into `HostCallPayload`
  - validates capability mismatch
  - performs policy + logging + timeout
  - dispatches by method with bespoke logic
- A shared dispatcher already exists (`dispatch_host_call_shared()`), but WASM hostcalls do not yet call it.

# Scope
1. Keep input validation that is specific to the WIT contract:
   - `name` must match `payload.method` (or explicitly document any tolerated mismatch).
   - JSON parse errors are `invalid_request`.

2. Replace the bespoke WASM dispatch path with:
   - Build `HostCallContext`:
     - `runtime = "wasm"`
     - `extension_id` (from registration state)
     - `policy`, `tools`, `http`, `fs`, `env_allowlist`, `manager`
   - Call `dispatch_host_call_shared()`.

3. Convert `HostResultPayload` to the WIT return type:
   - On success: return `Ok(<serialized output JSON>)`.
   - On error: return `Err(<serialized HostCallError JSON>)`.
   - Ensure error codes are taxonomy-only (shared dispatcher guarantees this).

4. Ensure logging is not duplicated:
   - `dispatch_host_call_shared()` already emits `host_call.start/end` and `policy.decision`.
   - Remove duplicate logging from the WASM entrypoint so we don’t double-count or drift.

# Tests
- `--features wasm-host` unit/integration tests:
  - capability mismatch -> `invalid_request`
  - denied -> `denied`
  - timeout -> `timeout` with retryable
  - session/ui/events with manager absent -> deterministic taxonomy
  - params_hash parity vs JS for canonical calls (tie into `bd-1uy.1.4`)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No duplicated policy/timeout/logging logic remains in the WASM hostcall entrypoint.
- [ ] WASM hostcall errors and success outputs match the shared dispatcher’s semantics.
- [ ] `cargo test --all-targets --features wasm-host` is green.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrightHeron","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:57:36.997165900Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T11:21:28.916105960Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T11:21:28.916075122Z","close_reason":"Completed: WASM host.call uses shared dispatcher; wasm-host gates green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extensions","hostcall","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-nom.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1uy.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:57Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-nom.1","depends_on_id":"bd-nom","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:57Z","created_by":"import"}]}
